q: What is the role of brown adipose tissue (BAT) in thermoregulation?
options: It stores lipids that are released as glucose when blood sugar drops
It acts as an insulating layer to prevent heat loss from organs
It performs uncoupled respiration, generating heat without producing ATP
It secretes leptin to signal satiety and reduce metabolic heat production
explanation: BAT contains uncoupling protein (thermogenin) that allows the proton gradient in mitochondria to dissipate as heat rather than driving ATP synthesis, generating warmth.
